title: Cleanup `buildah from` CLI
body:
Remove --image since image is an argument
Remove --mount, since I don't see a case where you would not want to do this.

comments.body: This also appears to be removing the option of specifying a container's name as a second argument?

@nalind Yes, you can do this via the --name option.  In our proposal we said the second argument is for mountpoint, but I am not sure that is a great idea.

Might rather just do

```
$ ctr=$(buildah from fedora)
$ buildah mount $ctr /mnt
$ dnf install httpd --installroot /mnt
```

I added back in the --mount option since it was blowing up the tests.  I think we need to consider how this should work.

If `builder from` Always mounts the image, then we would want 

```rootfs=$(builder mount $cid)```
To succeed if the $cid is mounted and return the rootfs, as well as mount the object and return the rootfs.
